core_payload.content:
# Symphony Project Workflow Safety

## 1. Role
Symphony Project Workflow Safety owns the delivery surfaces that keep implementation bound to repo law and operator constraints. It keeps workflow contract, approval and sandbox posture, night-shift gating, and testing or security guardrails together so automation remains review-safe in the codebase.

## 2. Workflow Safety Surface
This sub-hub groups `foundation.symphony.workflow-safety`, `symphony-workflow`, `night-shift-autonomy`, `testing`, `security`, `agent-control-plane`, and `ai-control-surface`. Together they define the law and visible controls that constrain the implemented orchestrator.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own assignment discovery, runtime continuity, or retry and status evidence. Those responsibilities belong to `assignment-intake`, `live-session-fabric`, and `run-observability`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Symphony Project Workflow Safety groups the repo-law and guardrail surfaces of the delivery stream: the matching foundation workflow-safety surface, Symphony Workflow, Night Shift Autonomy, Testing, Security, Agent Control Plane, and AI Control Surface. It keeps execution contract, operator controls, safety posture, autonomous-night gating, and verification guardrails together so implemented automation stays review-safe in the repository. This boundary matters because delivery quality depends on one explicit place where repo law and safety constraints are enforced in practice.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Symphony Project decomposition to isolate workflow law, approval posture, night gating, and verification guardrails from intake and live session fabric.
